column6: Clear summary of what was built and for whom; states current deployment or delivery state (is it running, where, how stable); lists any exclusions from the handoff and explains why; includes contact information for each team member.
column9: Overview is present but deployment status is vague, exclusions are not addressed, or contact information is missing.
column12: Overview is missing or too brief to orient someone new to the project.

column1: Setup and getting started: correctness and completeness
column6: Step-by-step instructions for getting the project running from scratch; lists all prerequisites (OS, runtime versions, tools, accounts); provides copy-pasteable commands for install, build, test, and local dev; covers how to deploy or links to a deployment runbook; includes common pitfalls and resolutions.
column9: Setup instructions are present and mostly complete but missing some prerequisites, commands, or steps a new person would need.
column12: Setup instructions are missing, incomplete, or inaccurate enough that they would not reliably work for a new person.

column1: Known issues, limitations, and future work
column6: Honest, prioritized list of open bugs (with tracker links or explicit list and severity), known limitations, identified technical debt, and a prioritized future work backlog; includes any credentials, secrets, or access permissions that need to be transferred; tone is honest, not promotional.
column9: Known issues and future work are documented but prioritization is absent, severity is missing, or credential/access transfer is not addressed.
column12: Section is missing or claims "no known issues" without evidence.

column6: Final requirements document and final technical design document are linked or attached; links are accessible and clearly labeled.
column9: One of the two documents is present, or both are linked but inaccessible or unlabeled.
column12: Both documents are absent or inaccessible.

column6: Checklist is complete; all applicable items are checked or include a clear explanation and follow-up plan; written confirmation from the project partner (or instructor/mentor substitute) is included and demonstrates that they can access the repository, run the project, and understand future maintenance needs.
column9: Checklist is included and mostly complete; partner confirmation is present but partial; some unchecked items lack explanation.
column12: Checklist is missing; partner confirmation is absent; or nearly all items are unchecked without explanation.

column6: Archive drop link is submitted; archive contains all significant artifacts from all three terms (requirements, design, ADRs, test plans, reports, presentations, meeting notes, demo videos, source code, and any other course artifact); a release consent declaration is included in the archive with every team member marked as consenting or not consenting.
column9: Archive is submitted but missing some artifact categories; or release consent declaration is present but not all team members are accounted for.
column12: Archive is missing, inaccessible, or clearly incomplete; release consent declaration is absent.

column6: Professional tone throughout; instructions are precise and unambiguous; code blocks used for all commands and file paths; all links are descriptive and working; screenshots and diagrams include alt text and captions.
column9: Mostly clear; minor formatting issues or broken links; minor gaps in code block usage or alt text.
column12: Unclear or unprofessional writing; missing code blocks; broken links; no alt text.
